commit.message:
Fix NRE in AddUniversalDynamicSymbol for invalid symbol address ranges (#2376)

When a ProcessSymbol event has an invalid address range (e.g.,
StartAddress=0x0, EndAddress=0xFFFFFFFFFFFFFFFF from zeroed
/proc/kallsyms on Linux without root), the ETLX conversion crashed
with a NullReferenceException.

Three fixes applied:
1. Skip symbols where EndAddress <= StartAddress (invalid range)
2. Change (int) to (long) cast for the size parameter to match
   ForAllUnresolvedCodeAddressesInRange's parameter type
3. Add null guard on loadedModule as defense-in-depth

Fixes #2373

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: two-tier enabled/disabled — global device on/off with separate BT release

Redesign the enabled flag into a two-tier system:

1. Global enabled (config, requires restart): when enabled=false, no
   SendspinClient, BluetoothManager, port, or MA registration is created.
   Device metadata stored in state for UI display.

2. BT Release/Reclaim (hot toggle): existing functionality, only
   available when globally enabled. Releases BT adapter to host OS.

- state.py: add disabled_devices storage (set/get with thread safety)
- sendspin_client.py: skip disabled devices entirely in main() loop
- routes/api_status.py: include disabled_devices in /api/status response
- routes/api_bt.py: add POST /api/device/enabled endpoint (restart_required)
- static/app.js: render disabled devices section, add Disable button
- templates/index.html: add #disabled-devices-section container

commit.message:
fix: set audio_streaming=True on stream start (not only format_change)

audio_streaming was only set True in _handle_format_change, which
doesn't fire on re-anchor or track change when the codec/format is
unchanged. After stop→start, audio_streaming stayed False even though
audio was flowing — causing red equalizer indicator despite working
audio.

Now also set audio_streaming=True on stream 'start' event when
audio_format is already configured (re-anchor, track change).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
Movement checks (#170)

* Replace wall collision penalty with proactive movement masking

- Add Room.can_link_move_from() for pixel-level movement checks using
  NES ObjGridOffset (tile checks only fire at grid boundaries)
- Add ZeldaGame.can_link_move() with locked-door-with-key override
- Add link_grid_offset to data.json (NES RAM 94)
- Remove WALL_COLLISION_PENALTY and _did_link_run_into_wall from critics
- Replace reactive _handle_wall_bump/move_mask in action_space with
  proactive per-direction can_link_move() calls in the mask builder
- Remove prevent_wall_bumping parameter from ZeldaActionSpace
- Replace stuck_count>=8 heuristic in frame_skip_wrapper with
  deterministic can_link_move_from() check at movement start
- Update tests: wall collision test → movement masking test,
  adjust stalfos injury timing for fewer wasted frames

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix movement masking: always check tiles regardless of grid_offset

ObjGridOffset is axis-agnostic — a nonzero offset from vertical movement
was incorrectly allowing horizontal moves into walls. The tile check is
conservative and never produces false positives, so always apply it.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Always build Room from current RAM tiles to fix stale door corridors

ZeldaGame.room now always calls Room.create() with current_tiles instead
of preferring the cache via Room.get(). Room.create() short-circuits with
torch.equal() when tiles haven't changed, so the common case is cheap.

This fixes stale corridor tiles when doors open (key used or enemies
cleared) — the cached Room previously retained the locked-door tile
layout, causing movement masking to block valid corridor movement.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Allow movement into cave entry tiles for proactive masking

Cave top tile (0xF3) is above OW_FIRST_UNWALKABLE but the NES triggers
a cave transition via CheckPassiveTileObjects (Z_07.asm:2957-2962) when
Link walks into it.  Treat it as walkable for movement masking so the
agent can enter caves.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Restrict locked-door-with-key override to doorway corridor

The NES CheckDoorway (Z_05.asm:3755) only fires when Link's
perpendicular coordinate matches the doorway: X=$78 for N/S doors,
Y=$8D for E/W doors.  Without this position check, any room with a
locked door would allow movement toward the door from anywhere in the
room, even when blocked by walls/blocks (e.g. debug_1_45.state).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Fix empty wavefront when exit seed is blocked by obstacles

The wavefront used a single center exit tile offset off-screen as its
BFS seed.  If obstacles blocked expansion from that one point, the
wavefront never reached the rest of the room (e.g. debug_0_68.state).

Two fixes:
- Use ALL exit tiles as off-screen seeds so the BFS can expand from
  every exit point, not just the center.
- Skip can_move checks when expanding from off-screen seeds since they
  are virtual positions with no real NES tiles.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* Update scenarios

* Fix pixel-alignment bug in can_link_move_from using exact NES hotspots

can_link_move_from was delegating to can_move which used approximate tile-
coordinate offsets (tr+1 for feet). The NES computes hotspot pixel positions
directly: base_Y = ObjY + $0B, then subtracts $40 for the status bar before
dividing by 8 to get the tile row.

The old formula (py - 56) // 8 + 1 disagreed with the NES formula
(py - 53) // 8 for 62% of Y positions (when (py-64) % 8 < 5), checking
one row too low. This caused false positives — allowing movement the NES
would block.

Rewrote can_link_move_from to compute exact NES hotspot positions:
- Left:  col=(px-8)//8,   row=(py-53)//8
- Right: col=(px+16)//8,  row=(py-53)//8
- Up:    col=px//8,        row=(py-61)//8,  checks col and col+1
- Down:  col=px//8,        row=(py-45)//8,  checks col and col+1

can_move (tile-space) left unchanged for wavefront BFS which is internally
consistent with its approximate offsets.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <175728472+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: prevent false zombie restarts when audio is actually playing

Zombie detection now only triggers when audio has NEVER arrived in
the current subprocess session. Brief streaming gaps (re-anchor,
group sync, track changes) no longer cause false positives — PA
buffers keep playing during these gaps so restarting is wrong.

Reverted timeout back to 15s since it now only applies to genuinely
stuck subprocesses that never received audio data.

commit.message:
feat(matter): resolve SBMD endpoints by device type matching (#174)

The original method used to locate the Matter device endpoint to use for
a resource originated from the method used by Zigbee. Zigbee doesn't
have the device types and parts list attributes which are much better
suited to locate endpoints hosting desired functionality. Our drivers
are for specific device types (on/off light, dimmable light, etc). This
maps directly to the device types attribute on Matter endpoints and is
more reliable than just looking for an endpoint with a target cluster as
before.

- Add endpoint map (sbmdEndpointMap) to MatterDevice, built during
AddDevice() by matching device type lists against driver's
matterMeta.deviceTypes
- Add ResolveEndpointMap() and GetEndpointForSbmdIndex() methods
- Update BindResourceReadInfo, BindWriteInfo, BindExecuteInfo, and
BindResourceEventInfo to accept optional sbmdEndpointIndex parameter
- Pre-resolve Matter endpoint IDs at bind time; device-level resources
fall back to GetEndpointForCluster()
- Store resolvedEndpointId on write/execute bindings for use at
invocation time
- Update SpecBasedMatterDeviceDriver to pass endpoint index for
endpoint-level resources and std::nullopt for device-level resources
- Add MatterDeviceEndpointMapTest with 27 tests covering endpoint map
resolution, index lookup, and all four binding methods
- Add device-type-endpoint-resolution spec to openspec/specs
- Archive endpoints-from-device-types change

Refs: BARTON-358

---------

commit.message:
feat: smooth restart — mute sinks, cache sink names, auto-unmute

Three improvements for glitch-free service restarts:

1. Startup sink mute: daemon_process mutes PA sink before BridgeDaemon
   starts, _startup_unmute_watcher unmutes after audio_streaming=True
   + 1.5s stabilization delay (60s timeout safety)

2. Pre-restart local mute: saveAndRestart() mutes all local PA sinks
   via force_local before stopping — no MA pause/play needed, so sync
   group members on other bridges keep playing uninterrupted

3. Sink name cache: configure_bluetooth_audio() saves found sink name
   to LAST_SINKS[mac] in config.json; on restart tries cached name
   first, skipping 3s A2DP delay + retries if still valid

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>
